after_payload.conversation_reference:
[SES-036 reconciliation note] This session record exists at SES-036 because the styling Conversation 2 originally rebased its anticipated SES-028 identifier to SES-030 based on the database snapshot showing SES-029 as the latest applied session. Between that rebase commit (7255667 on 05-16-26) and Doug's run of the apply prompt (05-18-26), a separate v0.5 follow-up conversation independently claimed SES-030 on 05-17-26 by direct API POST without committing a ses_030.json file. When Doug ran the styling Conversation 2's apply prompt, the script's POST returned 409 on SES-030 and absorbed it as SKIP (the script's documented idempotent behavior for partial prior runs). DEC-105, DEC-106, DEC-107 and four references DID insert successfully but their decided_in/is_about targets pointed to the v0.5 follow-up SES-030 record instead of the intended styling Conversation 2 record. This reconciliation payload (a) inserts the displaced session content at the next-available identifier SES-036; (b) re-inserts DEC-105/106/107 which the apply script will SKIP via 409 (already present); (c) adds four correct references (DEC-105/106/107 to SES-036 decided_in, SES-036 to PI-001 is_about). A separate cleanup step in the companion apply prompt issues DELETE /references/{id} for the four orphan references (ids 136, 137, 138, 139) that point at SES-030. Forensic conversation captured in this session's natural conversation_reference content below.

Claude.ai conversation on 05-16-26 that opened the styling workstream's build-planning phase against the design-pass document produced by SES-027 earlier the same day. The conversation operated against the project default ARCHITECTURE mode for PRD and implementation-plan authoring, then switched to DETAIL mode (per profile preferences for Claude Code prompt authoring) for the six slice prompts. The conversation produced eight deliverables across the session: ui-PRD-v0.6.md (430 lines), ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md (456 lines), and six slice prompts at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{A..F}-*.md (456 + 369 + 398 + 495 + 369 + 304 lines). Plus pre-flight corrections to PRD and implementation plan reflecting the actual v0.5 codebase shape, and an identifier rebase at close-out time reflecting v0.5 parallel-workstream consumption of anticipated identifiers (SES-028 → SES-030, DEC-095/096/097 → DEC-105/106/107). No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
after_payload.in_flight_at_end: v0.5 build is active in parallel — by close-out time v0.5 had advanced to its slice A prompt (engagement panel followup launcher wiring per CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-A-followup-launcher-wiring.md committed by v0.5 work) and consumed identifiers through SES-029 + DEC-104. The two workstreams remain coupled only at v0.5's engagement panel per DEC-076's boundary discipline; slice C's panel-retrofit work will pick up the engagement panel alongside the other 12 existing panels when v0.6 build executes. After SES-030 apply lands, v0.6 build executes via the six slice prompts in sequence (A→B→C→D→E→F, strict ordering, no parallelism within v0.6). v0.5 ships first per DEC-105; v0.6 ships when its six slices complete. Status entity versioned-replace from 'v0.5 complete' to 'v0.6 complete' is operator-authored after slice F lands. The paper-test (DEC-077) stays deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement record exists — unchanged by v0.6 work.
after_payload.title: Styling Conversation 2 — build planning: ui-PRD-v0.6, implementation plan, six slice prompts, version-bundling resolution
after_payload.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: not a formal kickoff document — this conversation opened against the styling design pass committed earlier the same day (styling-design-pass.md, output of SES-027) plus the workstream plan at styling-workstream-plan.md. Doug initiated by saying 'lets do the styling conversation' after the prior turn had reconstructed which conversation produced what (he had been confused which conversation gave him 'this script' — turned out to be the styling design pass document, not a script; the conversation had been searching past chats to disambiguate).

Pre-flight. Sparse clone of crmbuilder repo with CLAUDE.md, PRDs/, and crmbuilder-v2/ source. Read CLAUDE.md (684 lines) to ground architecture and conventions. Read styling-workstream-plan.md (227 lines) end to end. Confirmed no styling-conversation-2-kickoff.md existed — working from the workstream plan and design-pass document directly. Verified database state at conversation open: latest session SES-027, latest decision DEC-094, latest PI PI-017. Next anticipated identifiers: SES-028, DEC-095/096/097. (This identifier anticipation later proved wrong; see close-out section.)

What Conversation 2 owed per the workstream plan: (1) settle the version-bundling question — v0.5 bundled vs v0.6 separate; (2) produce a styling release PRD; (3) produce an implementation plan; (4) produce slice build prompts.

Decision 1 — Version bundling (DEC-105). Pause with full eight-element template. Two viable options: bundle the styling work into v0.5 (one release for engagement management + visual design system) versus ship as separate v0.6 release. Option A (bundle) gave one closeout cycle and slice-name collisions could be managed via prefix; Option B (separate) gave two closeout cycles but kept release versions tied to functional themes (the v2 history-navigation index that 'what changed in v0.5' answers cleanly). Doug chose B (separate v0.6). Reasoning: release versions are v2's primary navigation index (git log, README, decisions table, __version__, MCP-exposed status); v2 to date had held one clean functional theme per release (v0.1 storage, v0.2 base CRUD, v0.3 references + sessions write surface, v0.4 polish + methodology panels); bundling would make v0.5 the first v2 release with two unrelated themes welded together, permanently blurring navigability. Cost: one additional closeout cycle (~half day of administrative work). The workstream plan author's weak prior leaned this way and was ratified. Follow-on detail committed in the same decision: PRD file ui-PRD-v0.6.md; implementation plan ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md; slice prompts under prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{A..F}-*.md; __version__ after v0.6 closeout '0.6.0'; v0.5 ships first (engagement is currently slightly ahead in planning depth); engagement panel coupling per workstream plan §4's 'v0.5 ships first' branch; design-pass decisions stand as-is.

Decision 2 — Six-slice structure (DEC-106). Surfaced as draft rather than full template (substantively a packaging choice rather than two genuinely different visual outcomes). The workstream plan §5.3 sketched five slices (Foundation / Governance retrofit / Methodology retrofit / Dialog polish / Closeout). Drafted six slices for v0.6: A Foundation + About dialog (canary surface exercising the full token system end-to-end); B Sidebar + master-pane delegate (the shared selected-state vocabulary); C Panel retrofits + ReferencesSection (all 12-13 panels in one omnibus retrofit slice); D Dialogs + form controls; E Status + crash banner (separated from dialogs because crash banner and status surfaces are distinct concerns from dialog chrome); F Closeout. Doug approved as drafted. Three reconciliation differences from the strawman: (1) master-pane delegate pulled to its own slice B rather than buried in panel retrofits — the delegate is shared across all 12+ panels and putting it in panel-retrofit slices would force artificial governance/methodology coupling; (2) governance and methodology retrofits collapsed into single omnibus slice C — the retrofit pattern is identical across all panels, and splitting repeats review work without buying anything once slice B has landed the shared delegate; (3) status surfaces + crash banner promoted to their own slice E — keeping two distinct concerns separately reviewable.

Decision 3 — Slice acceptance pattern (DEC-107). Drafted briefly rather than full template (visual work has different acceptance signals than functional work; the choice was between several reasonable approaches with similar value). Drafted pattern: per-slice after-state screenshot per affected surface, committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-screenshots/slice-{X}/ — captured by Doug after running each slice's Claude Code prompt, used for retrospective ('what did the sidebar look like at the end of slice B?') and as release-note material; plus eyeball verification by Doug against the design-pass document as primary acceptance signal; plus automated WCAG AA contrast check against codified styling.py values at slice F closeout per design pass A9 (the one accessibility commitment v0.6 makes — small Python script using a contrast-ratio library, parameterised over the combinations from design pass §4.4). Doug approved as drafted.

ui-PRD-v0.6.md drafted. 430 lines, format mirroring ui-PRD-v0.4.md. Header (Version 0.6, Last Updated 05-16-26 17:30, Status Draft — pending approval); change log; §1 Overview with Purpose / Background / Source decisions (the full deferral chain DEC-024 through DEC-076 plus the design-pass decisions DEC-087 through DEC-094 plus the three forthcoming decisions to be recorded); §2 Scope with 15 In Scope items and 9 Out of Scope items; §3 Architecture with layer responsibility table (5 new modules, 2 restructured widgets, 12+ panels retoken-touched); §4 Functional Requirements citing the design pass rather than restating values; §5 Cross-Cutting Concerns (version bump, README, test target, screenshot capture protocol); §6 Acceptance Criteria per-slice (A1-A7, B1-B7, C1-C7, D1-D6, E1-E6, F1-F7) plus cumulative roll-up; §7 Implementation Plan Reference with strawman reconciliation; §8 Constraints; §9 Risk Register with 9 risks; §10 Open Questions; §11 Decisions to Be Recorded. Doug approved as drafted.

ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md drafted. 456 lines, format mirroring ui-v0.4-implementation-plan.md. §1 Overview with strict A→B→C→D→E→F sequential dependency chain (no parallelism); §2 Implementation Choices including the hybrid token-consumption mechanism, font/icon asset bundling under ui/assets/, screenshot capture protocol per DEC-107, WCAG contrast check library choice; §3 Directory tree with ASCII layout showing new modules, restructured widgets, asset directories, and screenshot directory; §4 Build Sequence per-slice deliverables and acceptance gates cross-referenced to PRD ACs; §5 Migration Ordering (explicit 'None'); §6 Test Target (~8-12 new tests in slice F WCAG module; visual verification screenshot-based per DEC-107); §7 Screenshot Capture Protocol (six-step protocol for Doug); §8 Version Source; §9 Closeout Discipline. Doug approved as drafted.

Pre-flight corrections from slice A prompt-drafting. Before authoring slice A's prompt, investigation of the actual v0.5 codebase surfaced three factual errors in the implementation plan and PRD: (1) the v0.1-shipped ui/styling.py module already exists (DEC-024's minimal QSS stub that PI-001 has been tracking) and should be rewritten in place rather than replaced by a new tokens.py — surfaced with full eight-element template since two genuinely different options (rewrite-in-place vs new file + delete legacy) had meaningfully different outcomes; Doug chose Option 2 (rewrite in place) on user-preference grounds plus apply_stylesheet(app) entry-point preservation; (2) About dialog lives at ui/about_dialog.py at the ui/ top level, NOT ui/dialogs/about.py — there is no about.py in dialogs/; (3) ui/base/crud_dialog.py contains BOTH EntityCrudDialog and EntityCrudDeleteDialog (single file with both bases) plus a third dialog base discovered: ui/base/versioned_replace_dialog.py covers CharterReplaceDialog and StatusReplaceDialog. Per-dialog modal-backdrop hook count corrected: 3 base classes + 3 standalone QDialog subclasses = 6 file edits (the bases plus about_dialog.py, dialogs/error.py, dialogs/reference_delete.py). The implementation plan §3 directory tree and §4 slice A deliverables list were patched accordingly; both documents transitioned from 'Draft — pending approval' to 'Approved.'

Mode switch to DETAIL for slice prompt authoring. Per Doug's profile preferences, Claude Code prompt authoring is DETAIL-mode work ('one consequential thing at a time, full discussion, wait for approval before each next step'). Doug confirmed the switch.

Slice A prompt drafted (456 lines). Foundation + About dialog. Nine numbered steps: (1) ui/styling.py rewrite end-to-end with TOKENS dict and accessor/builder; (2) font asset bundling and app-startup loading via QFontDatabase; (3) Lucide icon asset bundling and runtime-tinting loader; (4) modal elevation infrastructure (QGraphicsDropShadowEffect helper + ModalBackdropOverlay widget with attach/detach functions); (5) per-dialog elevation+backdrop hooks across the six files identified; (6) ui/base/list_detail_panel.py token-consuming chrome hooks; (7) app.py startup wiring order; (8) splash screen migration from legacy constants to t() accessor; (9) about_dialog.py re-skin (wordmark + tagline + vertical metadata list). Two upstream architectural choices surfaced via the eight-element template before drafting: (a) token-consumption mechanism (Option A — programmatic QSS generation from styling.py keeping token integrity as a single source of truth, chosen over static QSS file + manual sync burden; follow-on detail: build_app_stylesheet(tokens) function in styling.py, applied in app.py at startup, t(key) accessor for per-widget reads); (b) modal backdrop visibility-coupling mechanism (Option 1 — per-dialog hook via showEvent/hideEvent overrides, chosen over QApplication.focusChanged or global event filter on explicitness/reliability grounds; follow-on: backdrop overlay parented to QMainWindow.centralWidget per PRD Open Question #3). Plus four routine decide-and-announce items: tagline wording ('Declarative CRM deployment and methodology authoring' per design-pass proposal); font file sources (rsms/inter and JetBrains/JetBrainsMono GitHub releases for variable-axis .ttf files); icon loader caching (lazy by (name, size, color_token) key, module-level dict, no invalidation); predecessor slice reference as placeholder ('v2-ui-v0.5-? TBD at v0.6 build-execution time'). Doug approved slice A as drafted.

Slice B prompt drafted (369 lines). Sidebar + master-pane delegate. Eight numbered steps culminating in the shared selected-state vocabulary per DEC-093. One upstream architectural choice surfaced via the eight-element template: master-pane delegate registration mechanism (Option B — centralized via ListDetailPanel base class with per-panel fallback for VersionedPanel-based panels Charter/Status, chosen over per-panel registration on 12 panels which would create a latent bug class for future panels; follow-on: ListDetailPanel.__init__ applies delegate automatically; Topics overrides via class attribute master_pane_delegate_cls = MasterPaneTreeDelegate). Plus three routine items: sidebar implementation via QStyledItemDelegate (per design pass + DEC-093); tree-view variant as subclass MasterPaneTreeDelegate(MasterPaneDelegate); soft-deleted toggle reading via is_soft_deleted_visible callable on the delegate constructor. Doug approved slice B as drafted.

Slice C prompt drafted (398 lines). Panel retrofits + ReferencesSection. Eight numbered steps including an explicit Step 1 inheritance-topology verification pass before any code, pause-and-report if any panel pattern unanticipated. One upstream architectural choice surfaced (brief framing, not full template): label-above form layout scope — slice C owns label-above EVERYWHERE (panels + dialogs via crud_dialog and versioned_replace_dialog bases) via QFormLayout.setRowWrapPolicy(WrapAllRows) plus a QFormLayout QLabel QSS rule in build_app_stylesheet, chosen over splitting between slices C and D. Plus four routine items: ReferencesSection refactored in place preserving public API; kind→label mapping as module-level constant in references_section.py; status combo Valid transitions caption as per-panel QLabel with QSS rule via objectName='statusHintCaption'; engagement panel filename verification (likely engagements.py plural per v0.5 test naming); required-field marker as required_label(text) helper in base/list_detail_panel.py. Doug approved slice C as drafted.

Slice D prompt drafted (495 lines — the largest slice prompt). Dialogs + form controls. Seven numbered steps: button helpers, button category QSS rules, combo dropdown + checkbox indicator, internal context strip, button-category migration across the codebase, delete-confirm body treatment, visual regression smoke. Decide-and-announce items rather than surfaced decisions: button category mechanism as helper functions in base/list_detail_panel.py (primary_button, destructive_button, text_link_button, icon_button) returning QPushButtons with buttonCategory dynamic property; context strip as method on EntityCrudDialog base auto-applied in edit mode only (excluded dialogs verified per design pass §2.7); checkbox custom indicator via QSS background-image referencing pre-tinted Lucide check.svg variants; inline error retoken already covered by slice A; combo dropdown via standard QComboBox QSS. Doug approved slice D as drafted.

Slice E prompt drafted (369 lines). Status, error, warning + crash banner. Six numbered steps including the WarningCallout widget for inline panel warnings, Processes panel retoken from danger-red to warning-amber, error dialog header retoken with Lucide circle-x icon, crash banner re-skin folded into the design system. Decide-and-announce items: WarningCallout as shared helper widget for reusability; crash banner button styling as per-widget setStyleSheet (the banner is exceptional and doesn't fit the five design-system button categories); error dialog header as layout restructure with leading icon row + title in heading-3 danger-text. Doug approved slice E as drafted.

Slice F prompt drafted (304 lines). Closeout. Five numbered steps: __version__ bump to '0.6.0'; README v0.6 release note; WCAG contrast test module using wcag-contrast-ratio library with hand-rolled fallback (parameterized over 8 combinations from design pass §4.4 A9); full regression test pass; final integration smoke checklist covering every surface across slices A-E. Recommended warning-amber token adjustment from #B0731A to #9D6517 if the warning-on-white combination fails AA at body size (closes PRD Open Question #4). Doug approved slice F as drafted.

Close-out artifact authoring. Per the SES-025/026/027 close-out pattern, this conversation's own close-out produces a JSON payload and apply prompt for application via apply_close_out.py. Pre-flight against the database snapshot at close-out time surfaced that v0.5 parallel work had consumed the identifiers this conversation anticipated: latest applied session SES-029 (not SES-027), latest applied decision DEC-104 (not DEC-094). Per the parallel-workstream coupling discipline in DEC-076, this conversation rebased: SES-028 → SES-030 (next available after v0.5's SES-029); DEC-095 → DEC-105, DEC-096 → DEC-106, DEC-097 → DEC-107 (next available after v0.5's DEC-104). Rebase applied across PRD, implementation plan, and all six slice prompts via sed; change-log entries added to PRD and implementation plan documenting the rebase. Same pattern as the SES-027 rebase from SES-026 that occurred during styling Conversation 1's close-out. ses_030.json (this payload) and CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-030.md authored alongside.

Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-A-foundation.md. This conversation built the foundational layer of v0.6 styling: the token system, asset bundling for fonts and icons, modal elevation infrastructure, and a re-skinned About dialog acting as a canary exercise of the new token plumbing end-to-end.

Token system rewrite in ui/styling.py replaced the 72-line v0.1 minimal QSS stub from DEC-024 with a full theme-keyed TOKENS dict (per DEC-089), a t() accessor, a build_app_stylesheet() generator, and an apply_stylesheet(app) entry point. The light theme codifies every value from styling-design-pass.md §1 (color, space, font, radius, border, shadow tokens). Legacy module constants (ACCENT_COLOR, ACCENT_HOVER, ERROR_TEXT_COLOR, DEFAULT_FONT_FAMILY, DEFAULT_FONT_POINT_SIZE) were removed; consumers in panels/engagements.py migrated alongside splash.py.

Asset bundling under ui/assets/ added Inter Variable v4.1 + JetBrains Mono Variable v2.304 per DEC-090 (OFL license bundled) and the initial 14-SVG Lucide icon wave per DEC-092 (ISC license bundled). The ui/icons.py loader exposes lucide(name, size, color_token) with runtime SVG color tinting and (name, size, color_token) caching. Modal elevation per DEC-091 landed as ui/elevation.py (apply_dialog_shadow helper using QGraphicsDropShadowEffect) plus ui/widgets/modal_backdrop.py (ModalBackdropOverlay with attach/detach module functions, fade-in via QPropertyAnimation on windowOpacity). Five modal dialogs received per-dialog hooks: crud_dialog (covers CRUD + CRUD-delete), versioned_replace_dialog (covers Charter + Status replace), about_dialog, dialogs/error, dialogs/reference_delete. list_detail_panel.py got chrome-only hooks (objectName, splitter handle, 45/55 default split) since it is not modal.

Notable finding: Inter Variable v4.x registers under the family name 'Inter Variable' rather than the expected 'Inter' — ui/app.py needs an Inter → 'Inter Variable' substitution after QFontDatabase loads bundled fonts; without it, the project QSS that references 'Inter' would fall through to the system fallback. About dialog re-skin per design pass §2.8 and DEC-094 restructured to wordmark + tagline header + vertical two-line metadata list + single right-aligned Close button; serves as the canary surface exercising the full token system end-to-end. test_about_dialog.py updated to walk the new vertical layout and to match the 'API base url' sentence-cased label. Full v2 test suite: 1236 passed, 1 skipped (v0.5 inventory remains green).

after_payload.title: v0.6 slice A follow-up — force Fusion style + explicit light palette to neutralize OS theme bleed-through
after_payload.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-A-followup-force-light-palette.md. This conversation diagnosed and fixed a defect surfaced during slice B visual review: on Linux Mint with a Cinnamon/GTK theme, Qt picked up GTK palette values for widget surfaces the project QSS did not explicitly cover (QLabel backgrounds, QWidget container backgrounds), so on a dark Mint theme the app rendered black backgrounds with near-black text — 'dark blue on black' — unreadable labels across the UI.

Diagnosis surfaced that simply changing the OS theme did not reliably fix it; some Mint 'light' themes still have dark palette roles, and Qt's GTK reading was sensitive to the specific theme structure. The root cause was that slice A's build_application() constructed QApplication, loaded fonts, and applied the project QSS but did not force a Qt style or palette. Without a forced style, Qt delegated to the platform-native style on Linux which read GTK palette values; without a forced palette, the default QPalette inherited dark theme roles where the OS theme provided them.

The surgical fix: force QStyle to Fusion (cross-platform Qt-native style that does not delegate to GTK or native platform styles), then force QPalette to an explicit light palette built from TOKENS['light'] so widget surfaces without explicit QSS rules render in design-pass light-theme colors. Palette role-to-token mapping mirrors design pass §1.2 color tokens; no design pass values are duplicated or drifted. After the fix, the application renders identically regardless of OS theme.

Notable findings: (1) the dark-on-dark failure mode was OS-theme-bleed-through, not a TOKENS values bug — the design pass palette was correct; it was the unstyled surfaces that needed explicit palette protection; (2) apply_stylesheet wraps the style in Qt's internal QStyleSheetStyle proxy, so a naive style().objectName() assertion sees 'QStyleSheetStyle' rather than 'Fusion' — the regression test test_style_is_fusion clears the project QSS before asserting on style().objectName() to expose the underlying base Fusion style. Intent of the test is preserved with this contortion documented in a comment. Five regression-test cases added in tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_app_palette.py: five palette role assertions plus the Fusion style assertion, with the WindowText/Window contrast case (the label-readability bug) explicitly tested as test_palette_window_text_is_dark_neutral. Test count: 1236 baseline + 5 new = 1241 passed, 1 skipped.

This conversation delivered the dialog + form-control vocabulary that defines v0.6's interactive surfaces: five button categories with full state coverage, helpers that wrap them, the internal edit-dialog context strip per design pass §2.7, and the migration of every panel's New + Delete + Add-reference + refresh CTAs.

Button categories landed in build_app_stylesheet additions: Primary (accent fill, white text), Secondary (transparent + neutral border — default), Destructive (danger fill), Text/Link (transparent + accent text), Icon-only (28x28 square, icon-only) — each with full state coverage (hover/pressed/disabled/focused). The buttonCategory dynamic property drives the QSS rules; default fall-through ensures a bare QPushButton renders as Secondary so no panel accidentally renders an unstyled button.

Button helpers in widgets/form_helpers.py wrap the categories: primary_button(text), destructive_button(text), text_link_button(text, *, icon_name=None), icon_button(icon_name, *, tooltip). Migration was systematic across the codebase: Dialog Save → primary_button via the CRUD base + replace base; Dialog Delete → destructive_button via CRUD-delete base + ReferenceDeleteDialog; per-record detail-pane Delete → destructive_button across 13 panels; panel 'New X' toolbar CTAs → primary_button across 13 panels; ReferencesSection 'Add reference' → text_link_button(plus); panel toolbar refresh → icon_button(rotate-ccw). Background color.danger.default; text color.neutral.0 white at font.size.body font.weight.medium; leading Lucide circle-alert icon at 16px white. Padding 12px x 16px (space.3 x space.4). Banner button styling kept as per-widget setStyleSheet (the banner is exceptional — semi-transparent white-on-color styling doesn't fit any of the five design-system button categories from slice D). setAutoFillBackground + palette ensure the danger background renders reliably on the top-level banner widget. Behavior unchanged: always-on-top, full window width, persists until dismissed or error resolved.

Inline form-field error verification: the QLabel[role='error'] QSS rule from slice A confirmed rendering correctly across multiple panels' validation flows. Test update: tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_processes_panel.py's soft-deleted-domain warning assertions look up the warning by its new WarningCallout widget class instead of QLabel; text content assertion preserved (the widget exposes text() with the message body). No schema changes; no API changes; v0.5 test suite remains green.

after_payload.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-F-closeout.md. This conversation mechanically completed the v0.6 release that discharges PI-001 after four prior deferrals (DEC-024, DEC-026, DEC-037, DEC-042 → DEC-076 reopening): version bump, README release note, WCAG contrast test as build gate, design pass §4.4 traceability fix.

Version bump: crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/__init__.py __version__ = '0.6.0'. Root pyproject.toml is the v1 distribution; the v2 version source per CLAUDE.md is __version__ only (About dialog reads via importlib.metadata for 'crmbuilder-v2' which is never installed as a standalone dist, so the __version__ fallback always wins). README v0.6 release note section added matching v0.5's format, placed above v0.5 in chronological-newest-first order. Highlights: tokens module (60+ tokens), bundled Inter + JetBrains Mono + Lucide assets, shared selected-state vocabulary, panel retrofit, dialog work, status surfaces (slice E), modal elevation, WCAG contrast verification, retired legacy colors.

WCAG contrast test at tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_token_contrast.py is the build gate per DEC-107: 8 text-on-background combinations from design pass §4.4 (A9) verified against WCAG AA threshold (4.5:1 for small body text); plus one helper-sanity test (black-on-white = 21:1, identical = 1:1) totaling 9 cases. Hand-rolled WCAG 2.x luminance + contrast implementation since the wcag-contrast-ratio PyPI lib is not bundled and the formula is short enough to inline.

Notable finding: the WCAG test surfaced two design-pass transcription errors that needed correcting. color.neutral.500: drafted '#7A8694' computed 3.71:1 on white (the design pass claimed 4.62:1) — failed AA at small body size; adjusted to '#6A7480' which computes 4.75:1 (passes AA, matches design intent). color.warning.default: drafted '#B0731A' computed 3.95:1 on white (the design pass claimed ~4.7) — failed AA at body size; adjusted to '#9D6517' which computes 4.88:1 (passes AA). The color.warning.default adjustment was the explicit recommendation in design pass §4.4 and PRD Open Question #4, validated empirically here.

Design pass update: §4.4 (A9) both adjusted ratios noted inline with their old drafted values for traceability; closing line now points at the slice F WCAG build gate as the authoritative verification. Test update: tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_about_dialog.py test_version_displays_* renamed and asserts '0.6.0'. Full regression: uv run pytest tests/crmbuilder_v2/ -v returns green across the entire suite (1250 passed, 1 skipped — the same v0.5 test inventory plus the 9 new WCAG cases). v0.6 IS RELEASED. PI-001 discharged. No schema changes; no API changes.

payload.active_work:
v0.6 (UI styling rollout) is shipped. __version__ is 0.6.0. PI-001 (full styling design pass per DEC-024) is discharged after four prior deferrals (DEC-024 → DEC-026 → DEC-037 → DEC-042 → DEC-076 reopening → discharge at slice F). All six slices A-F plus the slice A follow-up palette fix landed on 05-18-26 across seven Claude Code build-execution conversations (SES-037..SES-043).

Slice A (foundation + About dialog, commit 7e5c010 / SES-037) replaced the 72-line v0.1 QSS stub with the full token system: TOKENS dict (theme-keyed per DEC-089), t() accessor, build_app_stylesheet() generator. Bundled Inter Variable + JetBrains Mono Variable (DEC-090) and the initial 14-icon Lucide wave (DEC-092) at ui/assets/. Modal elevation (DEC-091) landed as ui/elevation.py + ui/widgets/modal_backdrop.py with per-dialog hooks across five modal dialogs. About dialog re-skinned per design pass §2.8 + DEC-094 as the canary surface.

Slice B (sidebar + master-pane delegate, commit 86d9072 / SES-038) delivered the DEC-093 selected-state vocabulary uniformly: 3px left accent bar + color.accent.subtle background + medium-weight text, applied identically to sidebar entries and master-pane rows across all 13 panels via centralized ListDetailPanel registration. Sentence-cased sidebar group headers; SidebarItemDelegate + MasterPaneDelegate + MasterPaneTreeDelegate; Topics tree-view chevron variant. VersionedPanel inheritance positive surprise — slice B's anticipated VersionedPanel fallback was no-work because Charter and Status inherit the delegate automatically as ListDetailPanel subclasses.

Slice A follow-up (force-light-palette fix, commit ad7df1b / SES-040, landed after slice C in commit order) is the surgical defect fix for a Linux Mint OS-theme bleed-through: dark backgrounds with near-black text rendered as 'dark blue on black' because slice A's build_application() didn't force a Qt style or palette. Fix: setStyle('Fusion') + explicit QPalette construction from TOKENS['light']. Five regression tests at tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_app_palette.py.

Slice C (panel retrofits + ReferencesSection sub-section rewrite, commit 0114324 / SES-039) delivered panel-level visual coherence: QFormLayout.WrapAllRows across 13 panels + crud_dialog + versioned_panel base, required_label helper with Lucide asterisk, status hint captions on classification combos, CollapsibleSection for Notes, and the in-place rewrite of widgets/references_section.py from v0.5's two-level Inbound/Outbound grouping to sub-sectioned plain-list rendering with kind labels.

Slice D (dialogs + form controls, commit a54adc5 / SES-041) landed five button categories (primary/secondary/destructive/text-link/icon-only) with full state coverage plus helpers in widgets/form_helpers.py. Migrated 13 panels' New + Delete CTAs, ReferencesSection's Add reference (text_link_button), toolbar refresh (icon_button). Internal edit-dialog context strip per design pass §2.7 (mono identifier + body name); delete-confirm body line-height + Destructive-right button order. Combo dropdown chevron + checkbox check pre-tinted Lucide SVGs.

Slice E (status, error, warning + crash banner, commit 296aa2a / SES-042) delivered the four small visible surfaces: new inline WarningCallout widget (Lucide circle-alert + warning.default); Processes soft-deleted-domain warning retoken'd off legacy #B22222 onto WarningCallout (warning-amber, not danger-red, because it's informational); error dialog header retoken'd off legacy #1F3864 navy onto Lucide circle-x + danger.default + heading-3; crash banner re-skinned onto color.danger.default + Lucide circle-alert + neutral.0 white text.

Slice F (closeout, commit 583f511 / SES-043) bumped __version__ to '0.6.0', added the README v0.6 release note, and landed the WCAG contrast test as build gate per DEC-107 (8 design-pass §4.4 combinations + 1 sanity case = 9 cases). The test surfaced two design-pass transcription errors: color.neutral.500 #7A8694 (claimed 4.62:1 but actually 3.71:1) corrected to #6A7480 (4.75:1); color.warning.default #B0731A (claimed ~4.7 but actually 3.95:1) corrected to #9D6517 (4.88:1) per design pass §4.4 + PRD Open Question #4 recommendation. Design pass §4.4 updated with corrected ratios and traceability notes pointing to the slice F build gate as authoritative. Full regression green: 1250 passed, 1 skipped.

Governance follow-up: this status update (version 16, replacing the misleading version 15) plus the seven SES-037..SES-043 build-execution session records are applied via the v0.6 build-execution closeout prompt at prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-v0.6-build-execution-closeout.md. The deferred slice screenshot captures (slice-B initial set already in working tree as styling-screenshots/slice-B/, untracked) land in subsequent operator commits.

The paper-test workstream (DEC-077) is fully unblocked. The next Claude.ai conversation can be the paper-test kickoff against a freshly-created CBM engagement via the single-gesture creation+activation flow shipped in v0.5 slice D.
